expectations.evidence: Message body is approximately 510 characters — under the 1000 char limit.

expectations.text: Contains at least one Voss label
expectations.evidence: 'It seems like running ops at a 20-person agency doing B2B SaaS work means you're carrying a lot of the coordination overhead...'

expectations.text: Contains a no-oriented ask phrase: 'would it be off base', 'is it a bad idea', 'would it be crazy', 'is this the worst timing', or similar
expectations.evidence: 'would it be off base to grab 20 minutes this week'

expectations.text: Does not use yes-oriented framing like 'do you want to', 'can we schedule', 'are you free'
expectations.evidence: None of these yes-oriented phrases present. Ask is no-oriented.

expectations.text: Specifies a short duration like '15 minutes' or '20 minutes'
Offers a low-friction no option ('if not, no worries', 'if it's a bad time, all good', or similar)
expectations.evidence: 'If it's not the right time, no worries at all - I'll leave this alone and stay in the network.'
No em dashes. All dashes are plain hyphens (space-hyphen-space).

expectations.text: Does not contain 'just following up', 'bumping this', 'circling back', or 'touching base'
Signals this is the last message in the sequence ('last note', 'last message from me', 'don't want to camp in your inbox', or similar)
expectations.evidence: 'Last note from me on this one - don't want to keep camping in your inbox.'

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. The Voss label is embedded inside the no-oriented ask (one long sentence) which is slightly unusual but valid.
